core.highest_question: Can the same protected judgment be recovered across implementations?

axioms.one_sentence: Cross-implementation decryption must recover identical payload bytes.
axioms.full_statement: A JavaScript-encrypted payload decrypts identically in every conforming implementation.
axioms.why: Interoperability requires one authenticated wire contract.

patterns.wrong: Each implementation can choose different associated data.
patterns.correct: Every implementation authenticates the same stable manifest coordinates.
patterns.why: Different associated data must fail authentication.

self_check: Did decryption recover the exact canonical payload bytes?
